metadata.title:
AMERICANS CUT DOWN USE OF
BEER BY TWO BILLION OR|NKs

pages.raw_text:
WASHINGTON, D. c., July I'll
AS result Of the prohibition wave
the actual quantity of beer and
whisky consumed in the United
States has decreased markedly dur
ing the last two years. Between
four and five million barrels of
beer marks the decrease for the
current year. The whisky figures
are not complete, but it is in pro-
portion to the beer decrease.


In 1907 the treasury department
collected it's dollar tax on 59,567,
S1S barrels of beer. With the fig
ures for one month still to come in,
the year of 1908 shows a consump.
ton of only 50,993,042 barrels. Els
Inmates for the month of June sug.
gest the addition of between four
and five million barrels for that
month. This makes the decrease
between four and mive million bar
rels, or more than billion pints.
The average drink of beer is half
a pint, so Americans may be said
to have taken some two billion
fewer drinks of beer in 1908.
